turns.user_message: My shoulders have felt like they are carrying a stack of wet bricks for a while now, and it just won't shake off today. I keep rubbing at my temples hoping the pressure will ease up, but it just sits there. Every little task feels heavier than it should, and I catch myself staring at the wall for way too long. I know I should push through, but my body is just dragging its feet. It is weird how a simple Tuesday can feel this heavy.

This tightness in my chest has been hanging around for weeks now, and it refuses to leave no matter how much I rest. I wake up with my jaw already clenched, and my lower back aches before my feet even hit the floor. I try to stretch it out, but the tension just snaps right back into place. It feels like my whole frame is locked in place, waiting for a break that never actually comes. I am just tired of carrying this invisible weight around everywhere I go.

My hands actually tremble when I try to focus, and my stomach drops like a stone every single time I sit down to start. The exhaustion has sunk so deep into my bones that even blinking feels like a massive chore. I feel completely hollowed out, like there is nothing left inside to fuel my movements. Every muscle screams at me to just stop, but I keep forcing myself forward anyway. I am running on absolute fumes and my body is finally staging a full rebellion.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by describing a physical feeling. This has been going on for a while.
